title: v0.1 Alpha Release
description: Shipped across the v0.1.x releases. Gate for making the repo public and sharing with coworkers. Language surface is honest — named arguments (#56), agents nest in modules (#63), types usable from agents (#70), enum variant construction completeness (#96), capability checks cover test blocks (#104) — schedule handlers fire on their own (#71), agent events survive crashes (#72), every spec example compiles or is marked Planned (#77), the first-five-minutes DX holds up (assertion failures say why #105, skein new git-inits #106, zsh completion #101, MCP compile_check tells the truth #109), and releases/badges/versioned docs are automated for public consumption (#100).

title: v0.2 Beta Release
description: Shipped as v0.2.0. Post-alpha hardening, informed by alpha feedback: replay backend injection (#73), the capability-parameter surface decision (#69) and parameter-scoped enforcement for process/timer/event.log (#57), process.spawn task bodies (#74), enum value-level exhaustiveness warnings (#76), local LLM backends for dev via OpenAI-compatible servers + skein.toml profiles (#107), LSP code actions from fix_hint/fix_code (#108), and the Int string-interpolation fix (#114).

title: v1.0.0-rc Release
description: The gate for tagging v1.0.0-rc.1 — output of the 2026-06-11 release-readiness pass. Shipped: every item fixed and v1.0.0-rc.1 tagged 2026-06-12. NOTE (2026-06-15 / 2026-06-19 roadmap resets): rc.1 was tagged prematurely. The 2026-06-14/15 dogfooding audit (skein-testing + FablePool ports) and the 2026-06-19 source-verified soundness/contract audit reset the GA picture — v1.0.0 is no longer an imminent RC promotion. The next release is the development milestone v0.4.0, not another RC. See docs/ROADMAP.md.

title: v0.4.0 — Truth & Soundness
description: First reset wave (1.0 blockers). Wave A — Truth reset & surface cuts: every public planning/spec surface accurately describes the current pre-1.0 state; remove the unqualified 'v1.0 frozen' banner; mark `via &stub`/`via Module` historical/superseded; document exact scenario-provider limitations; reconcile diagnostic ownership (E0028/E0029); de-reserve or justify `resume`; correct EventStore durability and dogfood-compat language. Wave B — Analyzer/codegen soundness: the 2026-06-19 audit found the earlier 'soundness mostly landed' claim was FALSE. Open blockers: B1 true `?` propagation (codegen does not early-return; Err type unchecked — `core_erlang.ex:1684-1710`), B2 eliminate escaping `:unknown` (`types_compatible?(:unknown,_)`/`(:json,_)` are universal; the claimed public-boundary guard at `analyzer.ex:3408` does not exist), B3 local/effect/callback argument typing (arity-only today; no callable type), B4 unknown-call/invalid-codegen rejection + analyzer⇒BEAM property, B5 total Option + nominal record representation (records are untagged maps; Option repr inconsistent across store/tool vs JSON), B6 tool & provider body return checking (tool output type discarded; scenario providers untyped; purity non-transitive). Exit: known soundness regressions are negative fixtures, exact diagnostics pinned, an accepted-program corpus loads and runs, no P0 soundness issue remains. The scenario-capability-environment RUNTIME authority (dynamic stack, SpawnContext) landed in #280-283; only its ANALYZER-side provider contract checking remains (tracked under v0.5.0 / #279).

title: v0.5.0 — Runtime Contract & Dogfood
description: Second reset wave (1.0 blockers). Wave C — Runtime ABI, schema, capability authority, storage honesty: make the runtime expose exactly the contract the analyzer and spec claim. Open blockers from the 2026-06-19 audit: C1 authoritative effect-ABI matrix / single source of truth (today three hand-maintained copies drift — `timer.cancel` is Result[String,String] in the analyzer, bare `:ok` at runtime, `-> ()` in spec); C2 one structured-error ABI (LLM/tool/provider errors leak raw Elixir structs behind opaque enums — `LlmError.ProviderError(..)`/`ToolError.ValidationError(..)` CANNOT match; store-put/memory-put are bare strings; blocked-live raises are intentionally uncatchable — document/freeze that); C3 one recursive schema engine (`llm.json[T]` does NOT validate, only parse+atomize; tool output never validated; uniqueItems/formats/nesting are dead); C4 scenario-provider analyzer soundness (runtime authority is DONE; analyzer provider contract type-checking + transitive purity are NOT — #279); C5 honest store contract (untyped dynamic store, dead Ecto typed-table path, id not Uuid-checked — decide typed-tables vs dynamic, generic String/Bytes PK — #255); C6 EventStore persistence + frozen shapes (SQLite is an orphaned parallel backend; ordinary append is ETS-only; durability claim is false; restart not genuinely tested). Wave D — Dogfood conformance & release gates (#262): CI compiles, loads, and RUNS reduced, pinned programs from Skein examples + skein-testing + FablePool-skein, asserts real Result/error/schema/store shapes, detects API drift (ambient `Uuid.new()` vs capability-gated `uuid.new()`), compiles every spec/docs `skein` fence (#202 subset), and snapshots complete structured diagnostics. Observability CLI work (#284 render/JSON/trace-crash fix) already landed. Minimal canonical substrate is NOT in this milestone — it is conditional on the FablePool-capable decision (see ROADMAP + the canonical-substrate decision issue); if kept it becomes a new `v0.6.0 — Canonical Substrate`, else it stays in v1.1.

title: v1.0.0-rc.2 — True release candidate
description: Cut a real RC only once every 1.0 blocker in v0.4.0 + v0.5.0 (and the conditional v0.6.0 canonical substrate, if the FablePool-capable promise is kept) is green and the conformance gate executes the reduced testbeds end-to-end. No feature work in this milestone — only freeze verification, blocker fixes, docs corrections, migration fixes, and release mechanics. This replaces the premature rc.1 framing: no new RC until the new 1.0 gate is close to green. Run the exact release-readiness workflow used for GA; soak the same candidate before promoting to v1.0.0.

title: v1.0.0 Release
description: The GA tag — NOT imminent (reset 2026-06-15, re-baselined 2026-06-19). Freeze only after all preceding contracts are executable and green. GA is a sound, honest, deterministic, dogfood-proven core: (1) sound analyzer/codegen/runtime (no analyzer-success crash/miscompute; no `:unknown` at public boundaries; true `?`; tagged records/Option); (2) typed/honest capabilities & effects with one effect-ABI matrix and one structured-error ABI; (3) scenario-scoped capability environments (analyzer + runtime); (4) deterministic/replayable scenario/golden testing; (5) one recursive schema engine across request/LLM/tool-in/tool-out; (6) honest store + EventStore contracts; (7) minimal canonical bytes/hash substrate ONLY if 'FablePool-capable' stays an explicit 1.0 promise (open decision); (8) dogfood conformance across Skein, skein-testing, and FablePool-skein as a continuous gate. Tags once v1.0.0-rc.2 has soaked and every blocker is closed. Application/memory policy (provenance, lineage, grants, redaction) stays OUT of the language.

title: v1.1: Hardening & Language
description: Post-1.0 language + effect work intentionally cut from the minimal 1.0 surface (default home for deferred items). Closures/anonymous functions (#248), record update (#251), module constants (#249), enum variant payloads (#247), `via`/`via Module` if still useful after scenario environments, behavioural stateful stubs, general effect-provider interfaces, content-addressed store convenience APIs (#255 convenience half), effectful crypto — secure RNG/keygen/signing (#257), `llm.rerank` (#145), docs/spec drift guards (#202), and the LSP structural-navigation work (#240). DEFAULT home for the conditional canonical-bytes/hash/encoding substrate (#245/#256/#246/#250): these stay here UNLESS the FablePool-capable decision keeps the 1.0 promise, in which case they promote to a newly-created `v0.6.0 — Canonical Substrate`. A specific item promotes to 1.0 only when a checked-in executable conformance program proves it indispensable.

title: v1.2: Interop & Agent Workflows
description: Bigger design efforts after the hardening wave: Erlang/Elixir FFI via extern (#141, explicitly cut from 1.0 — the 1.0 crypto route, if any, is a narrow internal `:crypto` wrapper, never general FFI), human-in-the-loop approval workflows with suspend before sensitive tool calls (#144), the browser-based trace viewer (#143), the Raxol TUI for an interactive CLI (#171, separately gated from the framework-neutral CLI work), and native structural search/codemod (#241).

title: Future: Platform
description: Deliberately deferred platform bets, parked until the core language and runtime stabilize: hot code upgrades (#142), the managed deployment platform (#148), and the tools/connectors marketplace (#149). Re-scope each from scratch when pulled into active work.
